===============================================

Today is February 15, 2026. During the Munich Security Summit, California Governor Gavin Newsom spoke about the future of the US presidency. What specific timeline did he provide regarding Donald Trump's tenure?
(A) He stated that Trump would be gone in three years.
(B) He predicted Trump would be out of office by the 2026 midterms.
(C) He suggested Trump would serve a full second term ending in 2029.
(D) He claimed Trump would be replaced within the next twelve months.
(E) He argued that Trump's influence would last for at least another decade.
explanation: In the article, Gavin Newsom is quoted as saying, "Donald Trump is temporary. He'll be gone in three years."
model_output:
Based on reports from multiple news sources about Gavin Newsom's speech at the Munich Security Summit, he explicitly stated that Donald Trump "is temporary" and "he'll be gone in three years." This matches option (A). While some reports mention midterms or other context, the specific timeline he provided aligns directly with that phrasing.

<final_answer>
(A)
</final_answer>

CITATIONS:
- [AP News] (https://apnews.com/article/gavin-newsom-donald-trump-aac5dff6adf8cc845ade1206f0900b45)[[1]](https://apnews.com/article/gavin-newsom-donald-trump-aac5dff6adf8cc845ade1206f0900b45)
- [The Hill] (https://thehill.com/policy/international/5738685-gavin-newsom-donald-trump-wrecking-ball-us-munich)[[2]](https://thehill.com/policy/international/5738685-gavin-newsom-donald-trump-wrecking-ball-us-munich)
- [ABC News] (https://abcnews.com/Politics/newsom-aoc-seek-boost-profiles-reassure-allies-munich/story?id=130121765)[[3]](https://abcnews.com/Politics/newsom-aoc-seek-boost-profiles-reassure-allies-munich/story?id=130121765)

===============================================

Today is February 15, 2026. According to monitoring group TankerTrackers.com, the oil tanker Veronica III departed Venezuela on a significant date. On what day did it leave, and what other major event occurred that same day?
(A) It departed on December 31, the same day the US blockade was officially announced.
(B) It departed on 3 January, the same day US forces captured Nicholas Maduro.
(C) It departed on January 14, the same day the Aquila II was first tracked.
(D) It departed on February 1, the same day the Pentagon issued its "distance does not protect you" statement.
(E) It departed on January 3, the same day Chevron suspended its operations in the region.
explanation: The article states that the Veronica III departed Venezuela on 3 January, which was the same day the US captured Venezuelan president Nicholas Maduro.
model_output:
Based on the web search results, the oil tanker Veronica III departed Venezuela on January 3, 2026[[1]](https://www.bbc.com/news/articles/c4ge763d7j6o?at_campaign=rss&at_medium=RSS)[[2]](https://www.military.com/daily-news/2026/02/15/us-military-boards-another-oil-tanker-indian-ocean-after-tracking-it-caribbean.html)[[3]](https://www.argusmedia.com/es/news-and-insights/latest-market-news/2771982-tankers-defy-us-blockade-in-venezuela). This was the same day that US forces captured Venezuelan president Nicholas Maduro[[1]](https://www.bbc.com/news/articles/c4ge763d7j6o?at_campaign=rss&at_medium=RSS)[[2]](https://www.military.com/daily-news/2026/02/15/us-military-boards-another-oil-tanker-indian-ocean-after-tracking-it-caribbean.html)[[4]](https://cbsaustin.com/news/nation-world/pentagon-us-targets-sanctioned-tanker-in-indian-ocean-tied-to-illicit-venezuela-oil-nicolas-maduro-president-donald-trump-department-of-war-pentagon-maritime-military-caribbean-sea-associated-press-tanker-trackers)[[5]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/2026_United_States_intervention_in_Venezuela). A separate search confirmed there is no evidence of Chevron suspending operations on that date (in fact, US sanctions on Venezuelan oil were eased in mid-February 2026, allowing broader operations[[6]](https://www.reuters.com/business/energy/us-allows-oil-majors-resume-venezuela-operations-broadly-okays-new-energy-2026-02-13)[[7]](https://www.advocateanddemocrat.com/news/national/article_7bc11535-a87a-5a0d-8312-a48555705ec0.html)).

<final_answer>
(B)
</final_answer>

Today is February 15, 2026. The LA28 board recently reviewed Casey Wasserman's past interactions with Jeffrey Epstein. What did they determine regarding his 2003 flight on Epstein's private plane?
(A) It was a business trip to negotiate talent contracts for the upcoming Olympics.
(B) It was a personal vacation to the Caribbean arranged by Ghislaine Maxwell.
(C) It was a humanitarian mission to Africa at the invitation of the Clinton Foundation.
(D) It was a political fundraising trip for the Democratic National Committee.
(E) It was a private flight to London to meet with high-profile musical artists.
explanation: The LA28 board statement said Wasserman and his then-wife flew on a humanitarian mission to Africa on Epstein's plane at the invitation of the Clinton Foundation in 2003.
model_output:
The sources consistently indicate that the LA28 board reviewed Wasserman's interactions with Epstein (including a flight on his plane) and determined that the flight was a humanitarian mission to Africa at the invitation of the Clinton Foundation. While the question specifies 2003, all available reporting points to the flight occurring in 2002; this appears to be a minor discrepancy in the query, but the details match option (C) exactly.

<final_answer>
(C)
</final_answer>

CITATIONS:
- ESPN [web:9] (https://www.espn.com/olympics/story/_/id/47928858/la-28-olympics-boss-wasserman-sell-agency-amid-epstein-links)

Today is February 15, 2026. In the initial DHS account of the January 14 incident in Minneapolis, what specific weapons did they claim the migrants used to attack the federal officer?
(A) A metal pipe and a heavy chain.
(B) A kitchen knife and a blunt wooden club.
(C) A brick and a discarded car part.
(D) A snow shovel and a broom handle.
(E) A hammer and a length of industrial rope.
explanation: The DHS initially claimed the officer was attacked with a "snow shovel and broom handle" by individuals coming out of a nearby apartment.
model_output:
The incident appears to have taken place on January 14, 2026, involving Venezuelan migrants and ICE/DHS officers in Minneapolis. Based on available reporting, the initial DHS account (released the next day) claimed that the attack on the federal officer involved a snow shovel and a broom handle. Later accounts and evidence have reportedly contradicted this, but the question is specifically about the initial DHS claim.

<final_answer>
(D)
</final_answer>

CITATIONS:
- [DHS] (https://www.dhs.gov/news/2026/01/15/dhs-releases-more-details-about-three-violent-criminal-illegal-aliens-who-violently)[[1]](https://www.dhs.gov/news/2026/01/15/dhs-releases-more-details-about-three-violent-criminal-illegal-aliens-who-violently)
- [CNN] (https://www.cnn.com/2026/02/15/us/ice-shooting-dhs-doj-false-statements)[[2]](https://www.cnn.com/2026/02/15/us/ice-shooting-dhs-doj-false-statements)

Today is February 15, 2026. Former FBI hostage negotiator Chip Massey commented on the search for Nancy Guthrie's abductor. What did he say regarding the suspect's use of a mask?
(A) That the mask made it impossible for the public to provide useful tips.
(B) That the mask was likely a professional-grade tactical disguise.
(C) That the mask would be found discarded within two miles of the home.
(D) That the mask suggested the kidnapper was an amateur seeking quick cash.
(E) That while a mask can conceal a face, it cannot conceal a person.
explanation: Chip Massey stated, "while a mask can conceal a face, it can't conceal a person," referring to the suspect's behavior and body language.
model_output:
Based on web search results, former FBI hostage negotiator Chip Massey commented on the masked suspect in the Nancy Guthrie abduction case, expressing optimism that the kidnappers would be caught. Specifically, he noted that while a mask can conceal a face, it cannot conceal a person[[1]](https://www.bbc.com/news/articles/c5y48j0exlgo), implying that other identifiable traits (like build, gait, or clothing) could still lead to tips from the public. This aligns closely with option (E) and does not match the other choices.

<final_answer>
(E)
</final_answer>

CITATIONS:
- [BBC] (https://www.bbc.com/news/articles/c5y48j0exlgo)
- [AOL] (https://www.aol.com/articles/npr-host-compares-masked-guthrie-195953571.html)
- [Times Now] (https://www.timesnownews.com/world/us/us-news/nancy-guthrie-case-masked-man-bitcoin-demand-mother-of-today-show-host-savannah-guthrie-fbi-latest-updates-article-153616963)

Today is February 15, 2026. Following the release of a controversial video on President Trump's social media, Senator Tim Scott provided a specific reaction. How did he describe the video?
(A) As the most racist thing he had seen out of this White House.
(B) As a regrettable mistake by a junior staff member that required an apology.
(C) As a distraction from the real issues of voter fraud in the 2020 election.
(D) As a typical example of the "clown show" happening on social media.
(E) As an unfortunate lapse in decorum that undermined the office of the presidency.
explanation: Senator Tim Scott, the only black Republican senator, described the video as "the most racist thing I've seen out of this White House."
model_output:
Based on recent news reports from early February 2026, Senator Tim Scott reacted strongly to a racist video posted on President Trump's Truth Social account, which depicted Barack and Michelle Obama as apes. He specifically described it as "the most racist thing I've seen out of this White House."[[1]](https://www.axios.com/2026/02/06/tim-scott-trump-obama-ape-video)[[2]](https://www.foxcarolina.com/2026/02/06/sen-tim-scott-calls-trump-post-about-obamas-most-racist-thing-ive-seen-out-this-white-house)[[3]](https://www.newsweek.com/tim-scott-slams-donald-trump-for-video-featuring-obamas-as-monkeys-11478080) This matches option (A) directly, while the other options do not align with the reported statements.

<final_answer>
(A)
</final_answer>

CITATIONS:
- Axios (https://www.axios.com/2026/02/06/tim-scott-trump-obama-ape-video)
- FOX Carolina (https://www.foxcarolina.com/2026/02/06/sen-tim-scott-calls-trump-post-about-obamas-most-racist-thing-ive-seen-out-this-white-house)
- Newsweek (https://www.newsweek.com/tim-scott-slams-donald-trump-for-video-featuring-obamas-as-monkeys-11478080)

Today is February 15, 2026. Regarding the mass shooting in Tumbler Ridge, RCMP deputy commissioner Dwayne McDonald described the suspect's behavior during the attack. What specific term did he use?
(A) He described the suspect as "mentally unstable and erratic."
(B) He stated the suspect was, for a lack of a better term, "hunting."
(C) He characterized the attack as a "targeted domestic dispute."
(D) He referred to the suspect's actions as a "premeditated political statement."
(E) He called the suspect a "lone wolf with no clear objective."
explanation: Dwayne McDonald stated, "This suspect was, for a lack of a better term, hunting," noting they were engaging anybody they came in contact with.
model_output:
The mass shooting in Tumbler Ridge, British Columbia, occurred on February 10, 2026, at Tumbler Ridge Secondary School. The shooter was identified as 18-year-old Jesse Van Rootselaar, who killed eight people (including two relatives) before taking her own life. Based on available reporting, RCMP deputy commissioner Dwayne McDonald specifically described the suspect's behavior as hunting down victims indiscriminately.

<final_answer>
(B)
</final_answer>

Today is February 15, 2026. During the current partial government shutdown, Democrats have demanded specific reforms within the Department of Homeland Security. Which of the following is one of those specific demands?
(A) A total defunding of the US Coast Guard's non-lifesaving missions.
(B) The immediate resignation of Homeland Security Secretary Kristi Noem.
(C) A ban on immigration agents wearing face masks.
(D) The repeal of the "One Big Beautiful Bill Act" passed last year.
(E) An increase in the minimum wage for TSA agents working without pay.
explanation: The article mentions that Democrats have asked for a number of changes, including "a ban on immigration agents wearing face masks."
model_output:
I reviewed recent news about the partial government shutdown affecting the Department of Homeland Security (DHS) in February 2026. Based on reports, Democrats have pushed for specific reforms as a condition for funding, including restrictions on immigration agents (such as ICE) to improve accountability and identification. One key demand that matches the options is a ban on agents wearing face masks to conceal their identities, stemming from incidents involving federal agents and protesters.

<final_answer>
(C)
</final_answer>

CITATIONS:

Today is February 15, 2026. Mikhail Shaidorov won the gold medal in men's figure skating at the Milan-Cortina Games. This was Kazakhstan's first Winter Olympic gold in how many years, and who won the previous one?
(A) 10 years, since the 2016 Youth Olympics.
(B) 21 years, since Shaidorov was born.
(C) 28 years, since the Nagano 1998 Games.
(D) 32 years, since Vladimir Smirnov won in 1994.
(E) 40 years, since the 1986 Winter Games.
explanation: The article states this was Kazakhstan's first Winter Olympic gold in 32 years, since Vladimir Smirnov won the men's 50km cross-country skiing in 1994.
model_output:
Based on the available information, Kazakhstan's only previous Winter Olympic gold medal was won by Vladimir Smirnov in cross-country skiing at the 1994 Lillehammer Games[[1]](https://isu-skating.com/figure-skating/news/shaidorov-gives-kazakhstan-first-figure-skating-gold-as-hot-favorite-malinin-falters). Shaidorov's win in 2026 marks the country's first such gold in 32 years[[2]](https://www.reddit.com/r/popculturechat/comments/1r4tztx/mikahil_shaidorov_after_his_gold_medal).

<final_answer>
(D)
</final_answer>

(C) Julio Cesar Sosa-Celis.
(D) Alex Pretti.
(E) Renee Good.
explanation: The article states, "Sosa-Celis was shot in the leg during the incident."
model_output:
The Minneapolis shooting incident involving ICE agents occurred on January 14, 2026, and involved Venezuelan immigrants Alfredo Alejandro Aljorna and Julio Cesar Sosa-Celis (also referred to as Julio Sosa Celis in some reports). According to ICE's initial account, the two men assaulted agents with a broom and shovel, leading one agent to shoot Sosa-Celis in the leg. Subsequent investigations revealed that the agents' statements were untruthful, leading to them being placed on leave. Other options like Renee Good and Alex Pretti were involved in separate fatal or non-specified shootings, but Sosa-Celis is the one explicitly noted as shot in the leg in this incident.

<final_answer>
(C)
</final_answer>

CITATIONS:

(D) Around $2,400.

model_output:

Today is February 15, 2026. The US labour market has seen a significant slowdown in hiring. According to the report, what was the average number of jobs added per month in the US last year?
(A) 45,000 jobs.
(B) 150,000 jobs.
(C) 5,000 jobs.
(D) 115,000 jobs.
(E) 15,000 jobs.
explanation: The article states, "Last year the US added an average of just 15,000 jobs a month."
model_output:
The US jobs report for January 2026 (released in February 2026) included benchmark revisions to 2025 data, showing that the average monthly job growth for 2025 was 15,000 (with a total of 181,000 jobs added for the year). This aligns with the described slowdown in hiring.

<final_answer>
(E)
</final_answer>
